applicationcollaboration.title: ApplicationCollaboration
applicationcollaboration.description: Aggregate of application components working together

applicationcomponent.title: ApplicationComponent
applicationcomponent.description: Modular, deployable, and replaceable part of a system

type.description: Classification of the application component kind.

applicationevent.title: ApplicationEvent
applicationevent.description: A state change in an application element that triggers reactive application behavior.

eventType.description: Classification of the application event: domain (business logic), integration (cross-system), or system (infrastructure/runtime).

applicationfunction.title: ApplicationFunction
applicationfunction.description: Automated behavior performed by an application component for internal purposes, not directly exposed as a service to external consumers.

applicationinteraction.title: ApplicationInteraction
applicationinteraction.description: Collective application behavior performed by two or more application components working in collaboration, representing their joint behavioral contribution.

pattern.description: Interaction pattern used by this application collaboration.

applicationinterface.title: ApplicationInterface
applicationinterface.description: Point of access where application service is available

protocol.description: Application-level communication protocol used to expose the service. Use application-level protocols only; network/transport-level protocols (TCP, TLS) belong in the Technology Layer.

applicationprocess.title: ApplicationProcess
applicationprocess.description: An ordered sequence of application behaviors performed by an application component to achieve a specific operational result.

serviceType.description: Classification of how the service exposes its functionality to consumers.

dataobject.title: DataObject
dataobject.description: A passive application element representing data structured for automated processing by application components. Unlike behavioral elements, it is accessed, manipulated, or stored by active elements — not an actor itself. For canonical data schema definitions shared across layers, use the Data Model layer instead.
